subject: Computer Networking
title: SMTP: Sending Email
description: A 60-second lesson on SMTP: Sending Email.

hook.text: You hit Send on an email. Within seconds it reaches someone across the world. SMTP carries it there.

buildup.text: SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol — defines how email servers relay messages from sender to recipient.

discovery.text: Your mail client hands the message to your outgoing server. That server finds the recipient's server via DNS.

twist.text: SMTP only sends mail. It can't retrieve it. For reading, you need POP3 or IMAP — totally separate protocols.

climax.text: SMTP was created in 1982 with no authentication. Spam became rampant until extensions like SPF and DKIM arrived.

punchline.text: SMTP pushes email from server to server. That's all it does.

quiz.question: What is SMTP's primary function?
options: Sending and relaying email between servers
